name: Outbrain Click Prediction - 1st Place (2017) - Pipeline Template
summary: AdTech / Learning-to-Rank solution template for Outbrain Click Prediction - 1st Place (2017), modeled as a 5-stage deterministic pipeline: Data leak extraction, Feature hashing, Field-aware Factorization Machine (FFM), Follow-The-Regularized-Leader (FTRL), and downstream competition-specific finalization.
dejargonized_summary: This template captures the ordered steps used by Outbrain Click Prediction - 1st Place (2017): prepare the data, build task-specific representations, run the main modeling or search loop, and convert outputs into the final benchmark submission.

inputs.name: raw_competition_data
inputs.constraints: Raw data for a AdTech / Learning-to-Rank task.

outputs.name: solution_predictions
outputs.constraints: Final predictions formatted for the benchmark metric.

use_when: The new task has the same broad problem type: AdTech / Learning-to-Rank.
The available data supports the same ordered transformations and intermediate artifacts as the documented winning pipeline.
Offline processing is acceptable, including search, feature extraction, model ensembling, or post-processing stages.
do_not_use_when: The target task lacks the modality or metadata required by the early pipeline stages.
Strict real-time inference is required and the template depends on heavyweight extraction, search, ensembling, or test-time augmentation.
The evaluation metric rewards a fundamentally different output structure than the source competition.
applicability.key_insight: The load-bearing idea is the pipeline topology: Data leak extraction creates the representation needed by Rank Averaging, so later stages optimize the metric without solving raw-data routing from scratch.

swappable_stages.rank_averaging: The final model, search policy, or ensemble can often be replaced if it preserves the same input/output contract and metric semantics.

applicability.scaling_notes: Scale bottlenecks are usually in feature extraction, graph/retrieval stages, neural inference, solver loops, and ensemble evaluation; cache intermediate artifacts when adapting this CDG.
failure_modes: Distribution shift breaks handcrafted thresholds, validation-derived weights, or post-processing rules.
Missing metadata prevents faithful construction of candidates, groups, windows, graphs, or cross-validation folds.
A weak first-stage recall, search, or representation step cannot be fully recovered by downstream rescoring or ensembling.

planning_constraints.subject: preserve_stage_order
planning_constraints.statement: Pipeline stages must execute in the documented order unless a replacement stage preserves the same input/output artifact contract.
planning_constraints.rationale: Downstream stages assume artifacts produced by earlier transformations.

planning_constraints.subject: validation_leakage_guard
planning_constraints.statement: Splits, folds, time ordering, and group boundaries from the source task must be respected when adapting the template.
planning_constraints.rationale: Many winning pipelines depend on leakage-safe validation or temporal grouping.

stages.name: Data leak extraction
stages.description: algorithmically trace document IDs between page views and ad clicks, generating a strict deterministic matching feature that bypasses ML completely (pandas)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage algorithmically trace document IDs between page views and ad clicks, generating a strict deterministic matching feature that bypasses ML completely (pandas).

inputs.name: raw_competition_data
inputs.constraints: Raw inputs for Outbrain Click Prediction - 1st Place (2017).

outputs.name: data_leak_extraction_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Feature hashing
stages.description: hash billions of sparse categorical features (user IDs, geo-locations, ad IDs) into a fixed-width vector space to fit into memory (Vowpal Wabbit)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage hash billions of sparse categorical features (user IDs, geo-locations, ad IDs) into a fixed-width vector space to fit into memory (Vowpal Wabbit).

inputs.name: feature_hashing_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: feature_hashing_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Field-aware Factorization Machine (FFM)
stages.description: train an FFM architecture heavily optimized for sparse click-through-rate predictions (libffm)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage train an FFM architecture heavily optimized for sparse click-through-rate predictions (libffm).

inputs.name: field_aware_factorization_machine_ffm_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: field_aware_factorization_machine_ffm_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Follow-The-Regularized-Leader (FTRL)
stages.description: train an online FTRL model passing linearly through the dataset row-by-row, constantly updating its weights to handle chronological concept drift (Vowpal Wabbit)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage train an online FTRL model passing linearly through the dataset row-by-row, constantly updating its weights to handle chronological concept drift (Vowpal Wabbit).

inputs.name: follow_the_regularized_leader_ftrl_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: follow_the_regularized_leader_ftrl_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Rank Averaging
stages.description: sort the outputs of the FFM and FTRL models per user, and average their sorted ranks to generate final ad placements (scipy)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage sort the outputs of the FFM and FTRL models per user, and average their sorted ranks to generate final ad placements (scipy).

inputs.name: rank_averaging_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: solution_predictions
outputs.constraints: Final predictions or structured outputs for the competition metric.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

edges.provenance: Data leak extraction output is consumed by Feature hashing.
Feature hashing output is consumed by Field-aware Factorization Machine (FFM).
Field-aware Factorization Machine (FFM) output is consumed by Follow-The-Regularized-Leader (FTRL).
Follow-The-Regularized-Leader (FTRL) output is consumed by Rank Averaging.
